data.title: Malaysian Gold Follows Global Record Surge
data.description:
Gold prices in Malaysia surged to RM20,238.86 per ounce on Monday, marking a rise of RM257.18 from the previous session, as investors flocked to the safe-haven asset amid rising geopolitical tensions and a weaker US dollar.

The precious metal is now trading at RM650.69 per gram and RM650,693.97 per kilogram.

Globally, gold hit an all-time high of US$5,110.50 per ounce, extending a historic rally following a 64% surge in 2025, the largest annual gain since 1979.

Analysts point to safe-haven demand, US monetary easing, record central bank purchases and inflows into exchange-traded funds as key drivers behind the rally.

“We expect further upside. Our current forecast suggests that prices will peak at around US$5,500 later this year,” said Philip Newman, director at Metals Focus, per Reuters.

RHB Research highlighted that COMEX gold has now recorded four consecutive sessions of gains, with a bullish breakout above US$5,000 likely to open the door towards US$5,200.

Analysts advised traders to maintain long positions while monitoring support levels at US$4,550 and US$4,400.

Market observers noted that heightened uncertainty over US trade and global policy decisions has fuelled demand for gold, while a weaker dollar has made the metal more affordable for international buyers.

Spot silver also rose 4.8% to US$107.90 per ounce, alongside record levels in platinum and palladium.

In Malaysia, the gold market continues to reflect global trends, with year-to-date performance already up 4.21% for silver and a similarly strong trajectory for gold, as investors seek stability amid ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainties.

data.title: SGX Opens Higher, STI Gains 0.63% In Early Trade
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange (SGX) started the week on a positive note, with the Straits Times Index (STI) climbing 30.41 points, or 0.63%, to 4,891.34 in early trade.

Market activity showed 99 advancing stocks against 49 decliners, with a total trading volume of 68.15 million shares valued at S$147.22 million.

Leading the gains were blue chips DBS, which rose 0.76 points to S$59.05 (+1.3%), and UOB, trading at S$38.43. Singtel added ground at S$4.54, while OCBC Bank was steady at S$21.19. Shipbuilding stocks YZJ Shipbuilding and Nam Cheong also saw early buying interest.

In derivatives, equity index futures reflected steady activity, with the MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 459.55 and Nikkei 225 Futures at 52,915.00, while FX and commodity futures tracked moderate early movements.

The market opened firm amid cautious optimism from investors, following strong regional cues and a broadly positive start in Asia Pacific equities.

data.title: Asian Stocks Track Wall St Gains, Seoul Brushes Off Tariff Threat
data.description:
Asian markets rose Tuesday following gains on Wall Street, with tech firms leading Seoul to another record as investors brushed off Donald Trump’s threat to hike tariffs on South Korean goods.

The yen held its gains after a two-day surge stoked by intervention talk, while geopolitical and economic uncertainty saw silver hit another fresh peak and gold hover just below its own high.

Traders are also gearing up for a Federal Reserve policy meeting and earnings from tech titans, which will be pored over for an idea about sustainability of the AI investment surge.

Equities enjoyed healthy buying despite the US president reverting to tariff threats, warning South Korea he would impose 25 percent tolls on goods including autos for falling short of expectations on an earlier pact struck with Washington.

The announcement comes months after the two sides struck a trade and security deal following tense negotiations, setting levies at 15 percent.

“South Korea’s Legislature is not living up to its Deal with the United States,” Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform.

He added that he was increasing tariff rates “because the Korean Legislature hasn’t enacted our Historic Trade Agreement, which is their prerogative.”

The presidential office in Seoul said it had not been informed in advance but added that Trade Minister Kim Jung-kwan, currently in Canada, would head to Washington for talks with US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.

Trump’s outburst follows a warning to Canada on Saturday that it faced 100 percent levies if it signed a trade deal with China, days after backing down from a threat to hit several European countries with measures over their opposition to his grab for Greenland.

Still, Seoul’s Kospi continued its run to fresh record highs, with observers pointing to the US president’s history of rowing back the worst of his threats.

While carmakers slipped, tech firms ploughed higher with chipmaking giant SK hynix up more than five percent and Samsung Electronics up two percent.

There were also big gains in Hong Kong, Shanghai, Sydney, Singapore, Taipei, Manila and Jakarta.

Tech firms are enjoying a fresh boost ahead of earnings releases as traders continue to pile into all things AI.

Magnificent Seven members Apple, Microsoft, Meta and Tesla are due this week, with other bellwethers including Texas Instruments, Boeing and Mastercard providing an idea about the state of the economy.

However, with questions being asked about the amount of cash being invested in artificial intelligence, there is a little nervousness on trading floors about when profits will be realised.

“The AI capex cycle is increasingly colliding with the real world: debt markets, power grids, and regulation,” wrote Matt Weller, head of market research at City Index.

He added that “2026 capex estimates for the largest ‘hyperscalers’ is widely forecast to hit the $600 bn+ range, driven primarily by AI infrastructure. At the same time, major tech firms have leaned more heavily into debt issuance to fund the infrastructure race”.

“This matters for earnings because the market’s attention is moving from ‘who spends the most’ to ‘who can sustain the spend without eroding free cash flow’, especially if AI monetisation takes longer than expected.”

Developments in Washington are also being followed after some senators warned they would vote against upcoming spending bills following the second killing of a US citizen in Minneapolis, threatening another possible government shutdown.

The dollar remained under pressure after its latest selloff sparked by talk of a joint intervention between US and Japanese authorities to support the yen.

And in corporate news, Hong Kong-listed shares in China’s Zijin Gold International rose more than one percent after it agreed to buy Allied Gold, which owns gold mines in Africa, for US$4 billion. Its parent, Zijin Mining Group, soared more than six percent.

Zijin Gold’s shares have tripled since listing in September.

data.title: Weakening Dollar Lifts Chinese Currency
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, strengthened 86 pips to 6.9843 against the U.S. dollar Monday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day. 

The rise of regional currency, including the ringgit, has been attributed to the weakening dollar.

data.title: Decliners Edge Gainers As Market Movement Ends Mixed
data.description:
The stock market ended the trading session on Jan 26 on a soft note, with decliners narrowly outpacing gainers as trading sentiment stayed uneven across key sectors.

At the closing bell, the market recorded 617 losers versus 578 gainers, with 485 counters unchanged.

Malaysia Pacific Industries Bhd topped the laggards, falling 54 sen to RM32.58, while SAM Engineering & Equipment (M) Bhd shed 20 sen to RM3.50. ENRA Group Bhd slid 19 sen to 41 sen, Hong Leong Industries Bhd lost 16 sen to RM17.02 and Kelington Group Bhd eased 15 sen to RM5.30.

On the upside, Heineken Malaysia Bhd led the blue-chip gainers, rising RM1.10 to RM23.74, followed by Nestlé (M) Bhd adding 80 sen to RM116, Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd gained 78 sen to RM33.12, Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd increased 52 sen to RM18.34 and BLD Plantation Bhd surged 50 sen to RM15.80.

Overall, turnover was underpinned by pockets of institutional interest in consumer staples, while tech and industrial names saw profit-taking pressure, leaving the broader market in a marginally negative tilt by day’s end.

data.title: Crypto Weekly Wrap: Bitcoin, Ethereum Hover Near Key Levels Amid Consolidation
data.description:
Bitcoin traded in a relatively tight range this past week, consolidating around the US$87,000-US$90,000 area after rebounding from mid‑week lows. After opening near roughly US$92,000 on Jan 19, Bitcoin pulled back as global macro uncertainty and risk‑off sentiment weighed on crypto markets, then steadied around ~US$89,800 by Jan 23, reflecting sideways price action with compressed volatility.

Ethereum, likewise, experienced choppy trading, with Ethereum dipping from early‑week levels around US$3,200-US$3,340 before stabilising later in the session. Persistent outflows from major Ethereum spot ETFs and cautious positioning among institutional investors contributed to muted performance for the second‑largest crypto, even as modest stabilisation emerged by week’s end.

The week’s price dynamics underscored a broader market in consolidation, with traders awaiting fresh catalysts such as macroeconomic cues or renewed ETF flows to break the current range. Bitcoin’s compressed price bands suggest potential for a significant directional move in the near term, while Ethereum’s trading reflected lingering institutional caution.

Looking ahead, crypto traders will be watching for triggers such as macro data releases, regulatory developments, or shifts in ETF flows to catalyse the next significant move for Bitcoin and Ethereum.

data.title: Dollar Faces Pressure As Trump Policies And Geopolitical Risks Shake Investor Confidence
data.description:
The US dollar is under renewed pressure in early 2026 as investors reassess the impact of President Donald Trump’s policies and escalating geopolitical tensions.

On Monday, the dollar was set for its largest three-day slide against a basket of major currencies since April 2025, reflecting growing market uncertainty.

Trump’s aggressive trade stance, challenges to Federal Reserve independence and significant increases in public spending have all contributed to the greenback’s volatility.

So far this year, the dollar has lagged behind major rivals such as the euro, sterling and Swiss franc.

“There are a number of factors coming together and faster than expected,” said Seema Shah, chief global strategist at Principal Asset Management.

Market attention has been drawn to Trump’s threats on Greenland, European tariffs and trade tensions with Canada, alongside domestic issues including immigration crackdowns and the risk of a government shutdown.

Investors are also factoring in anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts, which make US assets less attractive relative to other markets.

Powell’s planned departure in May has further fueled speculation about his successor, with BlackRock’s Rick Rieder, a proponent of lower rates, now viewed as a frontrunner.

Meanwhile, global equities have outperformed US markets over the past year, with the S&P 500 rising around 15% since Trump’s inauguration compared with gains of 95% in Seoul’s Kospi, 40% in Tokyo’s Nikkei and nearly 30% in Shanghai’s main index.

“The trend is clear. Asset managers are keen to diversify away from the US,” said Chris Scicluna, economist at Daiwa Capital Markets.

Chris added, “Many had been overweight US markets and are now rebalancing.”

The dollar’s slide underscores mounting investor caution amid policy uncertainty and geopolitical risks, prompting a search for alternative safe havens such as gold and Japanese government bonds.

data.title: Stock Picks: IHH Healthcare, Pantech, Gas Malaysia And AEON Credit Service
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has highlighted several stocks showing bullish technical signals following recent breakouts above key resistance levels.

The research house said IHH Healthcare Bhd is extending its upside momentum after staging a bullish breakout, with the stock closing above the RM8.55 resistance.

RHB Research noted that the positive momentum could see the counter move towards RM8.95 and subsequently RM9.30.

However, the bullish setup would be negated if the share price falls below the RM8.20 support level, which could trigger a correction phase.

Pantech Group Bhd was also flagged after breaking above the RM0.69 resistance on strong volume.

RHB Research said the bullish candlestick formation indicates strong positive momentum, with the stock potentially testing RM0.73 before moving towards RM0.77.

A drop below the RM0.66 support would invalidate the bullish outlook.

Meanwhile, Gas Malaysia is seen approaching a potential continuation breakout after completing a consolidation phase.

RHB Research observed renewed buying interest as the stock moves closer to the RM4.55 resistance.

A decisive break above this level could lift the counter towards RM4.70, with a higher resistance at RM4.85.

The bullish setup would be nullified if prices slip below RM4.40.

Aeon Credit Service (M) was also cited as being in an upward trend after closing above the RM6.05 resistance.

RHB Research said the stock has remained above its 21-day simple moving average, signalling that the short-term trend remains bullish.

Following the breakout, the counter could advance towards RM6.40 and RM6.65, while a fall below RM5.80 would invalidate the current setup.

data.title: Wall Street Extends Gains Ahead Of Mega-Cap Earnings, Fed Meeting
data.description:
Wall Street edged higher on Monday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq logging their fourth consecutive session of gains as investors prepared for a wave of mega-cap earnings and a Federal Reserve policy update later this week.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 313.69 points, or 0.64%, to 49,412.40, while the S&P 500 gained 34.62 points, or 0.50%, to 6,950.23. The Nasdaq Composite added 100.11 points, or 0.43%, to 23,601.36. Both indexes hit their highest levels in over a week, marking their longest string of advances since December.

Big tech led the rally, with Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta and Broadcom providing the largest boosts to the S&P 500. Investors are closely watching these names for signs of measurable returns on AI spending, with upcoming quarterly results seen as a key test for the tech-driven rally.

“Communications and technology are trading well in advance of earnings from many large companies,” said Chris Zaccarelli, chief investment officer at Northlight Asset Management. “There’s cautious optimism ahead of earnings season.”

Meanwhile, individual sectors showed mixed performance. Communications services surged 1.3% to lead S&P 500 sectors, while consumer discretionary declined 0.7%, dragged down by Tesla, which fell 3%. The materials sector rose 0.3% amid gold prices topping $5,000 an ounce, boosting miners including Newmont Corp.

Shares of USA Rare Earth jumped 7.9% after reports that the U.S. government would acquire a 10% stake in the miner through a $1.6 billion debt-and-equity package. CoreWeave rose 5.7% following a $2 billion investment announcement from Nvidia.

Conversely, Intel fell 5.7% after forecasting quarterly results below expectations, and prison operators GEO Group and CoreCivic tumbled on political backlash over ICE funding.

Investors are also eyeing the Fed’s two-day meeting, where a hold on interest rates is widely expected, though market participants are looking for guidance on the central bank’s future rate path.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners on the NYSE and Nasdaq, with trading volumes slightly above the 20-day averages.

Reuters

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Jan 26, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on Jan 26, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: HSIF Holds Above Key SMA Lines, RHB Advises Longs
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its positive trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) after the contract posted a fourth consecutive rebound on Monday.

HSIF added 30 points to close at 26,748 points, after opening at 26,709 points. The index touched an intraday low of 26,625 points and a high of 26,929 points, forming a bullish Doji candlestick by the close. During the evening session, HSIF extended gains, rising 135 points to last trade at 26,883 points.

RHB Research noted that the relative strength index has turned higher, signalling an improvement in bullish momentum and giving the technical advantage to the bulls. With price action holding above the rising 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines, the broader market structure remains firmly in bullish territory.

If positive momentum persists, the research house said HSIF may retest the 27,400-point resistance. A decisive breakout above this level could propel the index towards the next resistance at 28,000 points.

RHB Research reiterated its recommendation for traders to retain long positions initiated at 21,416 points, set on April 14 2025. To manage downside risk, a stop-loss is fixed at 25,000 points.

The initial support levels are seen at 25,600 points and 25,000 points, while immediate resistance is pegged at 27,400 points, followed by 28,000 points.

data.title: Ringgit Strengthens To Seven-Year High Against US Dollar Amid BNM Hold
data.description:
The ringgit opened firmer on Monday, hovering near its strongest level in more than seven years following Bank Negara Malaysia’s decision to maintain the overnight policy rate and a weaker US dollar index.

At 8 am, the local currency was quoted at RM3.9850/9950 against the US dollar, up from RM4.0045/0080 recorded last Friday. The ringgit last traded near this level on 24 May 2018, when it touched RM3.9797 against the greenback.

Meanwhile, the ringgit eased against some major currencies. It weakened to 2.5680/5748 versus the Japanese yen, 5.4447/4584 against the British pound, and 4.7258/7377 versus the euro. It also dipped slightly versus the Singapore dollar and Thai baht but inched higher against the Indonesian rupiah and Philippine peso.

The stronger ringgit reflects improved risk appetite among investors following BNM’s decision to maintain its monetary policy stance and signs of softness in the US dollar.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Steady At 3.96 Against US Dollar As US-Japan Intervention Talks Intensify
data.description:
The ringgit opened steadily at 3.96 against the US dollar this morning, amid growing speculation of potential currency intervention by the United States and Japan.

At 8 am, the local note traded at 3.9650/9750 compared with yesterday’s close of 3.9615/9670, levels last seen in May 2018.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit could see further gains today, given the weaker US dollar.

“The Japanese yen strengthened against the US dollar as intervention talks intensified. Spot gold prices also climbed 1.04% to US$5,060.64 per ounce. This has translated into a softer US dollar, with the US dollar index (DXY) falling 0.57% to 97.040 points,” he told Bernama.

The ringgit showed mixed movements against other major currencies, weakening against the British pound to 5.4217/4354 from 5.4166/4241, easing versus the euro to 4.7076/7195 from 4.6999/7064, and slightly strengthening against the Japanese yen to 2.5683/5750 from 2.5783/5820.

It gained against the Singapore dollar to 3.1201/1282 from 3.1242/1288 and Thai baht to 12.6953/7343 from 12.7441/7679, slipped versus the Indonesian rupiah to 236.2/236.9 from 236.0/236.4, and remained almost flat against the Philippine peso at 6.72/67.4.

Dr Mohd Afzanizam noted that the ringgit’s upward momentum from yesterday could continue today amid a softer US dollar.

Bernama

data.title: HSIF Extends Rally, RHB Keeps Long Bias
data.description:
Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) reinforced its bullish setup last Friday after rising 72 points to close at 26,718 points, according to RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research), which reiterated its recommendation to retain long positions.

RHB Research said the index opened at 26,658 points and briefly dipped to an intraday low of 26,656 points before staging a strong rally to a high of 26,951 points.

The session ended with an inverted hammer candlestick pattern, signalling continued buying interest despite some intraday volatility.

During the evening session, HSIF added a further 54 points and was last traded at 26,772 points.

The research house noted that the firm price action above the 20-day simple moving average supports a resilient bullish tone, while the relative strength index moving above the 50% threshold points to improving upside momentum.

These technical signals suggest the index could continue trending higher in the near term, potentially testing the 27,400-point resistance level.

While RHB Research did not rule out short-term profit-taking, it said the broader bullish structure would remain intact as long as HSIF stays above the key 25,000-point support.

The firm maintained a positive trading bias and advised traders to continue holding long positions initiated at 21,416 points, with a stop-loss set at 25,000 points.

Immediate support is seen at 25,600 points, followed by 25,000 points, while resistance levels are identified at 27,400 points and 28,000 points.

data.title: Stock Picks: Leong Hup, IGB REIT, MISC And E&O
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Leong Hup International Bhd, IGB REIT Bhd, MISC Bhd and Eastern & Oriental Bhd as stocks with near-term technical upside following bullish price action and improving momentum.

Leong Hup International is showing signs of extending its upside movement after staging a bullish breakout on strong volume.

The stock closed above the RM0.80 resistance, signalling positive momentum. RHB Research said the counter may attempt to test RM0.85, followed by RM0.87.

However, a fall below the RM0.78 support could trigger a correction phase.

IGB REIT is eyeing further upside after testing immediate resistance amid strong volume. The stock printed a long upper shadow, suggesting the formation of an interim base.

A break above the RM3 level could pave the way for gains towards RM3.20 and RM3.40.

On the downside, a decline below the RM2.80 support would weaken sentiment and negate the bullish setup.

Meanwhile, MISC is setting up for a potential continuation of its bullish trajectory as it tests immediate resistance. RHB Research noted that the rising 21-day simple moving average indicates a strengthening trend.

A decisive close above the RM8 resistance could confirm a breakout, with upside targets at RM8.40 and RM8.60. A drop below RM7.80 would open room for a correction.

Eastern & Oriental is also poised to extend its bullish move after breaking past resistance with a strong white candlestick. The stock has climbed above the RM0.74 breakout point and formed a higher high, signalling strong positive momentum.

RHB Research expects follow-through buying to test RM0.79, followed by RM0.84, while a pullback below RM0.69 would invalidate the bullish outlook.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum Dip As Risk Aversion Grips Markets
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market is showing signs of pressure, with top-tier tokens Bitcoin and Ethereum posting notable losses on Jan 26 as traders reacted to a cautious macro environment and broader risk sentiment.

Bitcoin prices have weaken sharply, trading around the mid-US$86,000s, down roughly 3.2% over the past 24 hours, as market participants reduced exposure to risk assets and positioned into safer alternatives.

This decline weighed on Bitcoin’s dominant market share and underscored lingering macro uncertainties that have kept traders cautious.

At the same time, Ethereum also extended the downturn, slipping over 5% to the sub-US$2,800 range as bearish sentiment broadened beyond Bitcoin into major altcoins.

The steeper pullback in Ethereum highlights heightened volatility and profit-taking, with traders watching technical support levels closely amid range-bound price action.

On the other hand, the overall market breadth remained weak, with several mid- and large-cap tokens also in the red, even as emerging sectors such as Solana and other layer-1 platforms continued drawing narrative interest.

Analysts attribute the pullback to a broader macro-crypto divergence, where risk assets struggle amid global uncertainty and capital rotation into traditional havens. Investors continue to monitor ETF flows, institutional positioning, and regulatory developments as drivers for renewed momentum.

Moving forward, investors should weigh macro cues and market structure ahead of potential breakouts or deeper corrective phases, while rebounds above critical resistance thresholds may signal stabilisation and a return of risk appetite.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher, FBM KLCI Gains 8.77 Points
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher, led by early buying in blue-chip counters as investors appeared cautious but optimistic ahead of the corporate earnings season.

The benchmark FBM KLCI (FBMKLCI) climbed 8.77 points, or 0.50%, to 1,752.84, while the FBM70 added 25.92 points, or 0.15%, to 17,656.75. The F4GBM Index rose 4.29 points, or 0.42%, to 1,035.20, and the FBMEMAS index gained 51.37 points, or 0.40%, to 12,850.52. The FBMSHA edged up 20 points, or 0.16%, to 12,420.39.

Among the most active stocks, Greentec (7943) led trading with 8.34 million shares, gaining 1.5 sen to 25 sen. Capital A (5099) followed with 8.10 million shares at 59.5 sen, up half a sen.

One Gasmaster Holdings (0389), which made its ACE Market debut earlier, traded 4.54 million shares at 23 sen, down 2 sen from its opening.

Top gainers in early trade included CIMB (1023), up 13 sen to RM8.75, LPI Capital (8621) rising 12 sen to RM15.36, and AAX (5238), which added 11 sen to RM2.30.

On the flip side, UTDPLT (2089) slipped 14 sen to RM30.72, MPI (3867) fell 8 sen to RM32.50, and QL Resources (7084) lost 7 sen to RM4.18.

The early market sentiment suggests measured optimism, with investors weighing corporate earnings announcements against macroeconomic developments.

data.title: Bursa Kicks Off Week On A Positive Note, FBM KLCI Edges Up 0.17%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Monday, with the benchmark FBM KLCI rising 2.86 points or 0.17% to 1,722.85 at 9.06 am, led by gains in key blue-chip and mid-cap counters.

The broader market also saw positive movements, with the FBM70 up 38.82 points (+0.22%) to 17,601.04, the F4GBM rising 1.81 points (+0.18%) to 1,020.82 and the FBMEMAS gaining 24.78 points (+0.20%) to 12,685.32. FBMSHA rising 7.80 points (+0.06%) to 12,317.45.

Among the most active stocks, CapitaLand Malaysia Investment (CAPITALA) climbed 1.5 sen to 56 sen on 9.46 million shares, while Versatile Solutions (VS) slipped half a sen to 45.5 sen on 4.99 million shares. AAX Group rose 8 sen to RM1.92 with 4 million shares traded.

Top gainers included UTDPLT (+RM0.32 to RM30.82), Nestle Malaysia (+RM0.30 to RM115.50), HEIM (+RM0.16 to RM22.80), HL Bank (+RM0.14 to RM24.14) and PETGAS (+RM0.10 to RM18.68).

On the downside, IHH Healthcare fell 10 sen to RM8.50, TM lost 8 sen to RM7.88, UWC dropped 8 sen to RM4.17, Malayan Cement eased 7 sen to RM7.76 and Vitrox slipped 6 sen to RM4.49.

Market participants said early gains reflected cautious optimism among investors, although trading remained selective, with liquidity concentrated in mid- and small-cap stocks.

data.title: Bursa Hits 7-Year High As KLCI Gains 1.4%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its winning streak on Jan 26, with the FBM KLCI climbing 24.08 points, or 1.4%, to 1,744.07, marking its highest close since October 2018.

The surge was underpinned by strong buying interest in blue-chip stocks from both local institutional and foreign investors.

Other key indices also rose, with the FBM 70 adding 68.61 points to 17,630.83, FTSE4Good Bursa Malaysia up 11.9 points to 1,030.91, FBM EMAS gaining 138.61 points to 12,799.15 and FBM SHA rising 90.74 points to 12,400.39.

Top active counters included Capital A Bhd, which advanced 4.5 sen to 59 sen on 136.98 million shares, followed by AirAsia X Bhd, which gained 35 sen to RM2.19 and Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd, which increased 15 sen to RM7.56. SP Setia Bhd added seven sen to 95.5 sen, while Velesto Energy Bhd remained flat at 31 sen on 54.33 million shares changing hand.

Market watchers attributed the rally to renewed investor confidence in blue-chip stocks, propelling the benchmark to reclaim a seven-year high and reinforcing optimism for the Malaysian equity market in the year ahead.

data.title: SGX Opens Lower Amid MAS Policy Outlook, Semiconductor-Led Growth
data.description:
Singapore’s stock market opened lower on Monday as investors weighed robust economic growth against the prospect of the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) maintaining current monetary policy settings later this week.

At 9.06 am, the Straits Times Index (STI) stood at 4,879.25 points, down 12.20 points or 0.25% from Friday’s close, with 112 stocks advancing and 76 declining on a total volume of 153.81 million shares worth S$171.96 million.

Bank counters led the early weakness, with UOB down 2.66% to S$38.45. DBS, OCBC, CapitaLand Investment, Singtel, ST Engineering and YZJ Shipbuilding also traded lower in early deals.

Derivatives trading showed 29,340 contracts exchanged, with SGX FTSE Taiwan Index Futures at 2,586.75 and Nikkei 225 Futures at 53,065.00.

Market watchers said investor sentiment was influenced by expectations that MAS will keep monetary policy unchanged at its review on Thursday.

Singapore’s GDP expanded 4.8% in 2025, exceeding the government’s November forecast of around 4.0%, supported by strong semiconductor demand and a steady tech cycle, with the electronics PMI at 50.9 in December.

Economist Tay Qi Hang from the Economist Intelligence Unit Asia noted that AI-related demand and rising memory chip prices are likely to continue supporting the sector.

Standard Chartered chief economist Edward Lee said inflation remains under control, reducing pressure for immediate action, though MAS may tighten policy in April.

Bank of America economists flagged the possibility of an earlier tightening following December inflation data, which showed price increases in travel-related and other sectors offsetting declines in raw food and beverage prices.

MAS manages monetary conditions by letting the Singapore dollar trade within a band against currencies of its main trading partners, adjusting the slope, midpoint and width of the band as needed.

Globally, major central banks are expected to hold rates steady in the near term, although market uncertainty remains over the US Federal Reserve’s independence.
